mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% in Oct 2013 showing downtrend from 2009 peak
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal accelerating residential construction activity and forward-looking builder confidence, which increases demand for materials, labor, and consumer durables, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 891k units in Aug 2013, recovering from 2009 lows
failure_condition: When HOUST rises > 5% in 3 months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate combined with rising core inflation signals monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in Oct 2013, near zero
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and reduced capacity utilization, indicating slowing economic momentum that depresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.98 in Oct 2013, near 2017 baseline
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining unemployment rate signals strengthening consumer demand and labor market health, creating a virtuous cycle of earnings growth and equity price appreciation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 425.9B in Sep 2013, UNRATE at 7.3% in Oct 2013
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth signals strong labor demand and rising household income, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136.6M in Oct 2013, recovering from 2009 trough
failure_condition: When PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0.4%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth rate combined with rising retail sales indicates sustained economic expansion and consumer confidence, which supports equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15790.1B in Q2 2013, RSAFS at 425.9B in Sep 2013
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Very low federal funds rate near zero combined with rising inflation expectations signals monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through low real discount rates, but the mechanism reverses when FEDFUNDS rises above 2%, producing regime-dependent directional predictions for S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in Oct 2013, CPIAUCSL at 233.95 in Sep 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, SPX 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 63% of triggered instances, or the mechanism reverses (becomes negative) when FEDFUNDS rises above 2% in fewer than 70% of out-of-sample periods.
